properties.title: Aegina
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 59 A3 Aigina

properties.title: Amphipolis
properties.description: A Greek city in Thrace located on the east bank of the Strymon river, Amphipolis was originally founded as an Athenian colony.

properties.title: Anagnia
properties.description: Anagnia was a Hernican town that sided with Rome against the Volscians and later became civitas sine suffragio.

properties.title: Anazarbus
properties.description: Anazarbos or Anazarbus was an ancient city of Cilicia; its extensive multi-period archaeological remains are located adjacent to the modern village of Dilekkaya in the Kozan district of Turkey's Adana province.

properties.title: Ancona
properties.description: A port city founded by colonists from Syracuse in 387 BC.

properties.title: Ancyra
properties.description: An ancient settlement and later fortified settlement that served as the seat of the koinon of Galatia and capital of the subsequent Roman province. Modern Ankara in Turkey.

properties.title: Antium
properties.description: Antium ranked as one of the most important and most powerful centers of Latium and was captured by Rome at the close of the Latin War in the fourth century B.C. Imperial Antium was well known for its number of coastal villas.

properties.title: Apamea
properties.description: Pella/Apamea in Syria was an ancient city established by the Seleucids ca. 300 B.C.

properties.title: Apamea
properties.description: Apamea/Kelainai/Kibotos (modern Dinar) was an ancient settlement of Phrygia located at a road junction linking Ionia to the east. In 333 BC Alexander the Great installed his general Antigonos as satrap of Phrygia.

properties.title: Apollonia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B3 Apollonia

properties.title: Apulum
properties.description: Apulum (modern Alba Iulia) was an important Roman castrum in Dacia and the base of Legio XIII Gemina.

properties.title: Aquileia
properties.description: An ancient Roman city initially founded ca. 181/180 B.C., Aquileia is especially important in Late Antiquity and the Middle Ages.

properties.title: Aquinum
properties.description: Aquinum was a municipium by the time of Cicero. It was thought to be the place of birth for the satirist Juvenal and the emperor Pescennius Niger.

properties.title: Arae Flaviae
properties.description: Arae Flaviae (Rottweil) was founded as a Roman municipium in A.D. 73. by Cn. Pinarius Clemens.

properties.title: Arelate
properties.description: An ancient settlement located at modern Arles in France.

properties.title: Argos
properties.description: Argos was a city of ancient Greece that reached its cultural highpoint under Pheidon in the seventh century BC.

properties.title: Arretium
properties.description: Arretium (the Etruscan Aritim) was one of the duodecim populi Etruriae and described by Livy as one of the "Capitae Etruriae". The Romans captured the city in 311 B.C. and it became a station on the via Cassia.

properties.title: Arsamosata
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 B2 Arsamosata

properties.title: Artaxata
properties.description: An ancient city located on and around the hills known today as Khor Virap. This location is near the Armenian village of Lusarat, which is about 8 km south of modern Artashat.

properties.title: Ascalon
properties.description: Ashqelon/Ascalon was an ancient city in coastal Palestine, located midway between Azotus and Gaza. It became independent in 104 B.C. and remained the only free city in Palestine. Strabo refers to it, while Pliny calls it a free city (oppidum libera).

properties.title: Asculum
properties.description: Asculum (modern Ascoli Piceno) was the principal city of the Piceni.

properties.title: Astigi
properties.description: Now the modern Ecija on the Genil river, Astigi was the capital of one of the four conventus of Baetica and became an Augustan colony after 27 B.C.

properties.title: Asturica Augusta
properties.description: Asturica (Augusta) was the capital of the Conventus Asturum. An Augustan colony was established in 14 B.C.

properties.title: Augusta Treverorum
properties.description: The chief ancient city (civitas) of the Treveri. Modern Trier in Germany.

properties.title: Avaricum
properties.description: Avaricum/Biturigae, located at the confluence of Auron and Yèvre river, served as the capital of the Bituriges Cubi.

properties.title: Babylon
properties.description: The ancient city of Babylon began as an Akkadian city-state founded by Amorites in 1894 B.C. The city was also the seat of the Neo-Babylonian Empire from 608 to 539 B.C.

properties.title: Barcino
properties.description: Colonia Iulia Augusta Paterna Faventia Barcino (Barcelona) was a Roman settlement in Tarraconensis founded by Augustus.

properties.title: Beneventum
properties.description: Beneventum/Maleventum (modern Benevento) was an ancient city of Campania of Samnite origin.

properties.title: Berenice
properties.description: An ancient port city on the west coast of the Red Sea in modern Egypt.

properties.title: Berytus
properties.description: An ancient Phoenician city, known by various names in antiquity, in what is now Lebanon.

properties.title: Borysthenes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 E2 Borysthenes/Olbia

properties.title: Bracara Augusta
properties.description: With settlement from the Neolithic onwards, Bracara Augusta was established as a civitas in 20 B.C.

properties.title: Brixia
properties.description: An ancient settlement. Modern Brescia in Italy.

properties.title: Caere
properties.description: Caere (also known as Cisra or Cerveteri) was a major Etruscan polis located ca. 50 km north-northwest of Rome in southern Etruria.

properties.title: Caesaraugusta
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 E4 Col. Caesaraugusta

properties.title: Caesarea
properties.description: An ancient city in Pisidia.

properties.title: Capua
properties.description: An important ancient city of Campania with Villanovan and Etruscan origins.

properties.title: Carthago Nova
properties.description: Carthago Nova/Col. Urbs Iulia (modern Cartagena, Spain) was originally an indigenous settlement that was subsequently refounded first as a Punic site Qart Hadasht ("New City") in 228 BC and later as a Roman city.

properties.title: Casinum
properties.description: A Volscian hilltop settlement that became a Roman colony in 312 B.C.

properties.title: Chalcis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 F4 Chalcis

properties.title: Chersonesos
properties.description: Chersonesos was an ancient Greek colony founded in the southwestern part of the Crimean Peninsula, ancient Taurica. Settlers from Heraclea Pontica established the site in the sixth century B.C.

properties.title: Clunia
properties.description: Col. Clunia (Sulpicia) was a Roman city of Hispania Tarraconensis. The site was besieged by Cn. Pompeius Magnus during the war against Sertorius.

properties.title: Clusium
properties.description: Clusium, the Etruscan Clevsin (modern Chiusi), was a major city of Etruria. Lars Porsena was a sixth century B.C. king of Clusium.

properties.title: Cydonea
properties.description: An ancient settlement on Crete inhabited since the Neolithic period, Kydonia has been a major center of Minoan, Greek, and Byzantine civilization.

properties.title: Cyrene
properties.description: An ancient Greek city founded in eastern Libya ca. 630 B.C., Cyrene later became part of the Roman empire (ca. 74 B.C.). The site was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1982.

properties.title: Cyzicus
properties.description: An ancient city of Mysia in Anatolia.

properties.title: Damascus
properties.description: The ancient city of Damascus, located in modern Syria, with some 125 monuments dating back to the Roman period. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1979.

properties.title: Delphi
properties.description: The ancient pan-Hellenic sanctuary of Delphi in Greece, seat of the oracle of Apollo.

properties.title: Demetrias
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D2 Demetrias

properties.title: Diospolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 F2 Lydda/Diospolis/Georgioupolis

properties.title: Dyrrachium
properties.description: A city founded in 627 B.C. by colonists from Corinth and Kerkyra.

properties.title: Eburacum
properties.description: The site of a Roman legionary fortress, municipium, and colonia at modern-day York, England.

properties.title: Ecbatana
properties.description: The capital of Astyages (Istuvegü), captured by Cyrus the Great in 549 BC. The site served as a summer residence for both Persian and Parthian kings.

properties.title: Elateia
properties.description: Elateia was an ancient Greek city of Phocis, and numbered as the most important place in that region after Delphi.

properties.title: Eleutheropolis
properties.description: Govrin/Eleutheropolis was a Roman city founded on the site of Betaris.

properties.title: Elis
properties.description: Elis was an ancient Greek city of the northeast Peloponnese that served as the capital of the district of the same name.

properties.title: Eretria
properties.description: Eretria was an ancient polis located in Euboea.

properties.title: Europos
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the Euphrates river in modern Syria.

properties.title: Gabii
properties.description: An ancient Latin city located on the line of the Republican Via Praenestina connecting Rome to Praeneste. Gabii is allied to Rome from an early date and flourished during the archaic period, declining by the time of Augustus.

properties.title: Gades
properties.description: Gadeira/Gades/Col. Augusta Gaditana (modern Cadiz, Spain) is an ancient city of Spain and one of the oldest continuously occupied cities of Europe. Its origins may be Phoenician (Gadir (Phoenician: גדר)).

properties.title: Gaza
properties.description: An important ancient settlement on the coast of Palestine.

properties.title: Germe
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F3 (Hiera) Germe

properties.title: Hecatompylos
properties.description: Hecatompylos? was an ancient city in Iran that served as the capital of the Parthian Arsacid dynasty by 200 BC. Alexander passed through in 330 BC and the city eventually became part of the Seleucid empire and later a capital of the Parthian empire.

properties.title: Heliopolis
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley, named as a UNESCO World Heritage Site because "with its colossal structures, [it] is one of the finest examples of Imperial Roman architecture at its apogee."

properties.title: Heraclea
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the Black Sea coast of Asia Minor at modern Karadeniz Ereğli in Turkey. It is often referred to as Heraclea Pontica (or Heraclea in Pontus) to distinguish it from other cities with the same name.

properties.title: Hierapytna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 E2 Hierapytna

properties.title: Hippo Regius
properties.description: Hippo Regius (modern Annaba in Algeria) was a colony established by Phoenicians from Tyre as early as the twelfth century BC.

properties.title: Hispalis
properties.description: Hispalis/Col. Romula (modern Seville, Spain).

properties.title: Iulia Aug. Felix Germa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 G2 (Col. Iulia Aug. Felix) Germa

properties.title: Iulia Nobilis Cnosos
properties.description: A major ancient settlement of Crete located some 5 km southeast of Heraklion, Knossos was occupied from the Neolithic period to the time of the Roman empire.

properties.title: Lambaesis
properties.description: A Roman settlement established in the second century A.D. as the legionary base of Legio III Augusta

properties.title: Lanuvium
properties.description: Lanuvium was an important city of Latium located south of the Alban Hills. After opposing Rome at the battle of Aricia (504 B.C.) and Lake Regillus (496 B.C.), Lanuvium was captured by the Romans in 338 B.C.

properties.title: Laodicea ad Lycum
properties.description: "Laodicea on the Lycus": an ancient settlement located near the modern village of Eskihisar (Eski Hissar) in Denizli Province, Turkey.

properties.title: Larissa
properties.description: The ancient capital of the Thessalian Pelasgiotis, modern Larisa in Greece.

properties.title: Luca
properties.description: Luca (the modern Lucca) was originally an Etruscan settlement that became a Roman colony ca. 180 B.C.

properties.title: Lucus Augusti
properties.description: Lucus Augusti (Lugo, Spain). The Roman walls of the city were listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2000.

properties.title: Luna
properties.description: Luna (the modern Luni) is an ancient settlement of Liguria. It was re-founded by the Romans as a colonial settlement in 177 BC.

properties.title: Lyctus
properties.description: An ancient settlement on Crete, located just east of modern Xydas/Xidas, with which it is sometimes conflated. The city is mentioned in Homer and seems to have flourished in Hellenistic and Roman times, at least.

properties.title: Mantinea
properties.description: Mantineia/Antigoneia was an ancient settlement in Greece most noted for being the site of two major battles.

properties.title: Marcianopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 E5 Marcianopolis

properties.title: Massalia
properties.description: Massalia/Massilia (modern Marseille, France) was founded by Phocaean Greeks ca. 600 B.C. as a trading outpost.

properties.title: Megalopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C3 Megalopolis

properties.title: Megara
properties.description: Megara was an important ancient city of Greece, located in the northern part of the the Isthmus of Corinth opposite the island of Salamis.

properties.title: Melitene
properties.description: Melitene was an ancient city on the Tohma River. It was the station of Legio XII Fulminata from AD 70 onwards.

properties.title: Memphis
properties.description: A city and capital of Ancient Egypt that remained an important center for most of Egyptian history.

properties.title: Messene
properties.description: Capital of Messenia

properties.title: Miletus
properties.description: A major ancient city in Caria.

properties.title: Minturnae
properties.description: Originally an Ausonian town that fought against Rome in 314 B.C., Minturnae became a colony in 296 B.C. The site is located on the Garigliano river (ancient Liris) and served an important economic function. The Via Appia passes through the city.

properties.title: Naissus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 D6 Naissus

properties.title: Neapolis
properties.description: Founded as Parthenope by Greeks in the eighth century B.C., the site eventually gave rise to Neapolis in the sixth century B.C. and was one of the most prominent cities of Magna Graecia. Modern-day Naples, Italy.

properties.title: Nicaea
properties.description: Helikore/Nicaea (modern İznik, Turkey) was a Greek city of northwestern Anatolia. It was the site of the First and Second Councils of Nicaea and was the interim capital city of the Byzantine Empire between 1204 and 1261.

properties.title: Nisibis
properties.description: Nisibis/Antiochia was an ancient city of Mesopotamia that began as part of an Aramaean kingdom that the Assyrian king Adad-Nirari II captured in 896 BC.

properties.title: Nola
properties.description: The city of Nola (Nuvlana) is considered one of the oldest in Campania. Under Augustus the settlement became a Roman colony; it was the spot where Augustus died in A.D. 14.

properties.title: Oea
properties.description: Phoenicians established Oea in the seventh century B.C. and it later became a Roman–Berber colony. Modern-day Tripoli, Libya.

properties.title: Orchomenus
properties.description: The so-called "Boeotian Orchomenos", located in the vicinity of the Byzantine church of Panaghia Skripou.

properties.title: Ostia
properties.description: The port city of ancient Rome located at the mouth of the river Tiber.

properties.title: Palmyra
properties.description: An ancient oasis and trading city with monumental remains, located in modern Syria. Under Zenobia, Palmyra was the capital of the breakaway Palmyrene Empire, leading to Aurelian's razing of the city.

properties.title: Panormus
properties.description: First founded in 734 BC by the Phoenicians as Ziz, Pan(h)ormus (modern Palermo) was an important ancient city of the Greek, Roman, and Arabic civilizations.

properties.title: Patavium
properties.description: An ancient settlement of the Veneti, Patavium became a Roman municipium in 45 BC. It was the birthplace of several famous Romans including Titus Livius, Valerius Flaccus, Asconius Pedianus and Thrasea Paetus.

properties.title: Patrae
properties.description: Patra(e) (modern Patras) is an important ancient center with settlement beginning in the Late Helladic period. Patras played a leading role in the foundation of the second "Achaean League" and was the site of a Roman colony after 146 BC.

properties.title: Pax Augusta
properties.description: An ancient Roman city of Lustiania.

properties.title: Pella
properties.description: Capital of the Kingdom of Macedonia.

properties.title: Pelusium
properties.description: Pelusium was a fortified ancient city located at the mouth of the eastern-most branch of the Nile river. Its ruins, now land-locked and located approximately 30km southeast of Port Said, are known as Tell Farama.

properties.title: Pergamum
properties.description: An important ancient settlement, located at modern Bergama in Turkey. It was first described in Xenophon's Anabasis and was later used as a treasury under Lysimachus.

properties.title: Perge
properties.description: An ancient Greek city of Asia Minor and capital of Pamphylia.

properties.title: Perusia
properties.description: A city of ancient Umbrian and Etruscan origin, Perusia aided Rome during the Second Punic War but was sieged and reduced by Octavian in the first century B.C. during campaigns against Lucius Antonius.

properties.title: Petra
properties.description: Capital city of the Nabataeans located on the slope of Mount Hor.

properties.title: Phasis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Phasis

properties.title: Philadelphia
properties.description: An ancient settlement in northwest Jordan (modern Amman) received a Macedonian settlement and the name of Philadelphia from Ptolemy II Philadelphos (285-246 BC). The modern Amman, Jordan.

properties.title: Philippi
properties.description: An ancient city founded as Philippi by the king of Macedon, Philip II of Macedon on the site of the Thasian colony of Krinides.

properties.title: Philippopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Philippopolis/Trimontium

properties.title: Phraaspa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 90 C3 Phraaspa?/Phraata?/Vera?

properties.title: Pisae
properties.description: A city situated on the Tyrrhenian seaboard and likely of Etruscan origin. Its early nature was that of a mercantile center. The city became a Roman colony in 89 B.C.

properties.title: Pistorium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 41 D2 Pistoriae

properties.title: Placentia
properties.description: Placentia (modern Piacenza) was an ancient site with Gallic and Etruscan occupation before the Roman conquest of 218 B.C. The city persisted into Late Antiquity.

properties.title: Portus
properties.description: The great ancient maritime port of Rome, located on the right-hand bank of the Tiber river.

properties.title: Praeneste
properties.description: An ancient city of central Italy, the eighth and seventh century B.C. origins of which demonstrate high-level trade contact with the Eastern Mediterranean world.

properties.title: Ptolemais
properties.description: An ancient capital of Cyrenaica, Ptolemais was probably named after Ptolemy III Euergetes. The city was founded in either the seventh or sixth century BC.

properties.title: Puteoli
properties.description: A maritime city of Campania situated on the Phlegrean Peninsula. The settlement started as a Greek colony and became a Roman colony under the name Puteoli in 194 B.C.

properties.title: Reate
properties.description: Reate (modern Rieti) was a major Sabine center that came to be captured by Manius Curius Dentatus in late third century B.C.

properties.title: Romula
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B4 Romula/Malva

properties.title: Sabratha
properties.description: The westernmost of the "three cities" of Tripolis, Abrotonum/Sabratha was founded ca. 500 B.C. by the Phoenicians. The city was part of the Numidian Kingdom of Massinissa before becoming a Roman city.

properties.title: Sagalassus
properties.description: Sagalassos is an ancient city in Pisidia that was later part of the Roman province of Galatia. The city flourished until natural disasters and disease led to its decline in the sixth century AD.

properties.title: Salamis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Salamis/Konstantia

properties.title: Samosata
properties.description: An ancient city located on the West bank of the Euphrates river, established perhaps as early as the Hittite period. Its ruins, known in modern times as Samsat (Turkey), were submerged following the construction of the Atatürk Dam in the early 1990s.

properties.title: Sardis
properties.description: Sardis was the capital of the Lydian Empire located in western Turkey. In 188 B.C. Sardis became a part of the Attalid kingdom and thus it passed with the territory of Attalus III to Rome in 133 B.C.

properties.title: Scallabis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 B2 Scallabis/Praesidium Iulium

properties.title: Scupi
properties.description: Scupi (modern Skopje, Macedonia (Macedonian: Скопје)) has been the site of human settlement since ca. 4000 BC.

properties.title: Scythopolis
properties.description: A settlement located at the juncture of the Jordan and Jezreel valleys, Scythopolis/Nysa had important Egyptian through Roman phases.

properties.title: Sebastopolis
properties.description: A Greek city established by Miletos ca. 540 B.C. on the site of an earlier second millennium B.C. settlement.

properties.title: Seleucia
properties.description: A significant Hellenistic city that served as a capital of both the Seleucid and Parthian empires. The city was founded by Seleucus Nicator.

properties.title: Selinus
properties.description: A Greek colonial city located on the southern coast of Sicily, Selinus was said to have been founded as a colony of the city of Megara Hyblaia.

properties.title: Serdica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 F7 Serdica

properties.title: Sicyon
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 D2 Sicyon/Demetrias

properties.title: Side
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the southern coast of Asia Minor (modern Selimiye in Turkey).

properties.title: Sidon
properties.description: Sidon/Col. Aurelia Pia is an ancient maritime city and Phoenician metropolis

properties.title: Singara
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D4 Singara

properties.title: Sinope
properties.description: Sinope (modern Sinop, Turkey) on the Black Sea has been a major port since the time of the Hittites.

properties.title: Sirmium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 B5 Sirmium

properties.title: Siscia
properties.description: Segestica/Siscia is an Iron Age settlement recorded by Strabo. It was located at the confluence of three rivers and was used as a staging area for a campaign against the Dacians.

properties.title: Sitifis
properties.description: An ancient site at modern Sétif in Algeria, originally founded as a colonial settlement for Roman veterans during the reign of the emperor Nerva.

properties.title: Smyrna
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Asia Minor, modern Izmir in Turkey.

properties.title: Sopianae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 F3 Sopianae

properties.title: Sparta
properties.description: Sparta was a prominent city-state (polis) of ancient Greece.

properties.title: Spoletium
properties.description: An ancient city along the route of the Via Flaminia; an initial colony was established there in 241 BC.

properties.title: Tacapae
properties.description: Tacape (modern Gabès) was a commercial city located on the Lesser Syrtis.

properties.title: Tanais
properties.description: Tanais (located near modern Nedvigovska) was a city of the third century B.C. founded on the banks of the Tanais river by Bosporanian Greeks.

properties.title: Tarentum
properties.description: A Greek colonial city founded in 706 B.C.

properties.title: Tarracina
properties.description: A strategic Volscian city that had come under Roman control by 509 B.C.; a Roman citizen colony was established there in 329 B.C. The site is particularly notable for the remains of the terraced sanctuary of Iuppiter Anxur.

properties.title: Tegea
properties.description: Tegea was an ancient city of Arcadia. It is especially noted for its sanctuary dedicated to Athena Alea.

properties.title: Thebae
properties.description: The ancient city of Thebes in Boeotia (modern Greece).

properties.title: Theodosia
properties.description: A Greek colony founded by Miletus in the sixth century BC.

properties.title: Tibur
properties.description: A Sabine town, 30 km east-north-east of Rome, Tibur was the seat of the Tiburtine Sibyl.

properties.title: Tigranocerta
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Tigranocerta/Cholimma/Chlomaron

properties.title: Tripolis
properties.description: The Phoenicians established a commercial outpost at Tripolis (Tripoli) in the ninth century BC. An independent city during the Hellenistic period, Tripolis came under Roman control in 64 BC.

properties.title: Tyras
properties.description: A Black Sea colony founded by Miletus.

properties.title: Tyrus
properties.description: The ancient city of Tyre (modern es-Sur on the coast of Lebanon). A UNESCO World Heritage Site, in part because of its \"important archaeological remains, mainly from Roman times.\

properties.title: Verona
properties.description: Verona was an ancient settlement that became a Roman colonia in 89 BC and a municipium in 49 BC.

properties.title: Vienna
properties.description: A settlement of the Allobroges in Gallia Narbonensis that became a Roman colony in 47 BC.

properties.title: Vulci
properties.description: Vulci (originally Velch or Velx in Etruscan) was an Etruscan city 80 km northwest of Rome. Vulci was one of the duodecim populi Etruriae.
